items.prompt: set -e at the top of a script typically…
choices: Exits the script when a command fails (non-zero)
items.explain: errexit (-e) stops on the first failing command.

choices: It makes rm ignore the file
Spaces and special characters in the path stay one argument
It is required only for Python
items.explain: Unquoted expansions can word-split and hit the wrong paths.

items.prompt: A dry-run default (print what would be deleted) is safer than deleting on first run.
items.explain: Inspect first; require an explicit --confirm (or similar) for destruction.

items.prompt: ./clean_tmp.sh with no flags in this module’s example…
choices: Prints Would delete lines and leaves the files
items.explain: Dry run is the default; --confirm performs the deletes.
